reasoning: Lee Jae-myung was elected President in June 2025 and is highly popular within his Democratic Party (DP), which holds a comfortable majority in the National Assembly (~175 out of 300 seats). A presidential impeachment requires a two-thirds supermajority (200 seats), which means it would require a massive rebellion of nearly 100 DP lawmakers against their newly elected president within his first six months in office. While Lee face legal scrutiny prior to the election, the Seoul High Court indefinitely postponed his retrial in June 2025, citing presidential immunity. Without an active legal trigger and no new explosive scandals reported through August 2025, the likelihood of an impeachment motion both being proposed and passing by December 2025 is vanishingly small. The Polymarket price of 0.04 on August 21 accurately reflected this tail-risk-only reality, and the passage of time since then further depresses the probability to ~0.03.
